interaction.prompt: Power-play entry. Three PKers form a wall at the blue line. Tap which option you should use.

feedback.right: Drop pass back to the RD. PK is committed forward — pull them out, then enter behind their pressure with speed.
feedback.wrong: Both wingers are flanked by PKers. The PK is OVER-aggressive — the answer is to use it against them: drop to the late D.

tip: Power-play entry vs aggressive PK: drop pass + late attack. Don't force through three defenders.
why: 1-3 PK alignments cheat forward. Drop passes and late-D entries beat them because the PK's structure stops on the blue line.
